title: Argentina Designates CJNG as Terrorist Organization — Strategic Win for Sinaloa Cartel
detail: Argentine President Javier Milei's government designated the Jalisco New Generation Cartel (CJNG) as a terrorist organization on March 26, 2026, adding it to Argentina's national terrorism registry (RePET). The designation follows the February 2026 killing of CJNG founder Nemesio 'El Mencho' Oseguera Cervantes and marks a significant policy escalation in Latin America's approach to Mexican organized crime. Analysts note the move will pressure Argentine criminal networks tied to CJNG for cocaine transshipment routes to Europe, potentially benefiting rival Sinaloa Cartel operations in the Southern Cone. CJNG fighters had previously been deployed into Sinaloa to support Los Chapitos in the intra-Sinaloa Cartel war.

media.caption: Argentina designates CJNG as terrorist organization, strategic implications for Sinaloa Cartel

title: El Mayo Zambada Defense Sentencing Memo Due March 30 Ahead of April 13 Hearing
detail: As of March 26, 2026, El Mayo Zambada's sentencing remains pending in Brooklyn federal court. Defense attorney Frank Pérez must submit the sentencing memorandum and any objections to the presentence report by March 30, 2026, with the government's response due April 6 and sentencing rescheduled for April 13, 2026 before Judge Brian Cogan. Zambada faces a mandatory minimum of life in prison and a $15 billion forfeiture judgment after pleading guilty to leading a continuing criminal enterprise and RICO charges. His attorney has cited ongoing violence in Sinaloa as a hindrance to gathering witness testimony, but stated there is no cooperation agreement. US Attorney General Pam Bondi has called the case a 'landmark victory' for the DOJ.

media.caption: El Mayo Zambada defense sentencing memo due ahead of April 13 hearing
